name: pocketai-vscode
description: Use the local PocketAI desktop app from VS Code.

default.name: Local PocketAI
default.systemPrompt: You are a concise coding assistant inside VS Code.

url.description: Base URL for the PocketAI server.

model.description: Model ID to use with this endpoint. Leave empty to auto-detect.

reasoningEffort.description: Optional reasoning effort for endpoints that support it, such as the Codex Bridge.

maxTokens.description: Maximum completion tokens per reply for this endpoint.

systemPrompt.default: You are a concise coding assistant inside VS Code.

apiKey.description: API key / bearer token for this endpoint. Leave empty for unauthenticated local servers.

pocketai.endpoints.description: List of PocketAI server endpoints with per-endpoint model, token, and prompt settings.

pocketai.baseUrl.description: (Deprecated — use pocketai.endpoints instead) Base URL for the local PocketAI desktop app.

pocketai.includeWorkspaceContext.description: Automatically include a bounded snapshot of the current VS Code workspace and active file with PocketAI prompts.

pocketai.workspaceFileLimit.description: Maximum number of workspace files to include in PocketAI's file tree snapshot.

pocketai.currentFileCharLimit.description: Maximum number of characters from the active file to include in PocketAI context.

pocketai.projectInstructionsFile.description: Filename for project-specific instructions loaded into every conversation's system prompt.

pocketai.contextWindowSize.description: Context window size (in tokens) for your local model. Used by the token meter to show usage.

pocketai.maxContinuations.description: Maximum number of automatic continuations when the model hits its token limit mid-response.

allow.description: Glob patterns for auto-allowed tool calls, e.g. 'read_file(**)', 'run_command(npm run *)'

deny.description: Glob patterns for denied tool calls, e.g. 'read_file(*.env)', 'run_command(rm *)'

pocketai.permissions.description: Wildcard permission rules for tool calls. Deny rules take precedence over allow rules.

pocketai.useStructuredTools.description: Use OpenAI-compatible structured function calling instead of text-based tool parsing. Disable this if your model does not support function calling.

pocketai.includeGitContext.description: Automatically include git branch and status in workspace context sent with each prompt.

pocketai.includeDiagnostics.description: Automatically include VS Code diagnostics (errors and warnings) in workspace context sent with each prompt.

pocketai.useIntegratedTerminal.description: Run shell commands in VS Code's integrated terminal instead of a background process. Gives visibility into command execution and preserves shell state.

name.description: Unique name for this MCP server.

command.description: Command to launch the MCP server (e.g. 'npx', 'uvx', 'node').

args.description: Arguments to pass to the command.

env.description: Extra environment variables for the MCP server process.

enabled.description: Set to false to disable this server without removing it.

pocketai.mcpServers.description: MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers to connect to. Each server provides additional tools via JSON-RPC over stdio.

pocketai.hooks.description: Shell commands to run at lifecycle points. Supports ${file}, ${tool}, ${workspaceRoot} variables.
